steps.title: Match the singular subject
steps.content: The second clause has the singular subject TML. Its verb should match that subject and parallel the first clause, where DL pertains uses simple present tense.

steps.title: Reject plural forms
steps.content: A and C use plural auxiliaries, and D is a plural or base-form verb; all break agreement with singular TML. The sentence needs the singular simple-present form. The answer is B.
